name: Sentinel Hub API
what_it_does: Sentinel Hub provides cloud API access to petabytes of Copernicus Sentinel, Landsat, MODIS, and commercial satellite imagery with on-the-fly processing via custom Evalscript analysis scripts.
use_cases: Vegetation monitoring agents computing NDVI time series over agricultural fields using Sentinel-2 imagery
Flood detection pipelines generating SAR-based water extent maps from Sentinel-1 data after rainfall events
Climate research agents querying multi-year MODIS land surface temperature trends over specific regions
Change detection workflows comparing before/after imagery for disaster response or urban growth analysis
Environmental compliance agents monitoring deforestation, surface mining, or coastal erosion over time
not_for: Sub-meter resolution imagery — Sentinel Hub covers 10m-500m resolution; use commercial providers (Planet, Maxar) for sub-meter
Real-time streaming data — API provides on-demand access, not continuous satellite streaming
Offline or air-gapped environments — cloud-only processing platform
best_when: Building earth observation agents that need flexible, scriptable access to multi-source satellite data with on-the-fly band math, index calculation, or custom analysis without downloading raw imagery.
avoid_when: You need sub-meter resolution imagery, proprietary commercial satellite data not in the Sentinel Hub catalog, or fully offline processing pipelines.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow. Obtain client_id and client_secret from Sentinel Hub Dashboard. Token endpoint: https://services.sentinel-hub.com/auth/realms/main/protocol/openid-connect/token. Tokens expire after 1 hour.

pricing.notes: Processing Units (PU) are consumed per request based on output pixels and data collection. Sentinel-2 L2A costs 1-3 PU per request. Free trial available without credit card.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on plan; Basic: 300 process units/minute; rate limit headers included in responses

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns structured JSON errors with descriptive messages. OAuth token expiry returns 401 with clear message. Process API script errors include line numbers for Evalscript debugging.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All Process API requests are read-only and idempotent given identical parameters. Safe to retry on transient failures.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth tokens expire after 1 hour — agents must implement token refresh logic
Evalscript is a custom JavaScript DSL — agents generating scripts must learn the specific API
Processing unit consumption varies by request — agents should estimate PU cost before bulk operations
Cloud cover can return mostly empty imagery — agents should check cloud coverage metadata before processing
Coordinate systems must match the data collection — EPSG:4326 vs EPSG:32633 mismatch is a common error
Large area requests are automatically tiled — agents must handle multi-tile responses for AOIs > ~2500km²

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth 2.0 client credentials with short-lived tokens (1hr). TLS enforced. Client secrets should be stored securely. No PII in imagery data. GDPR compliant EU infrastructure.
